2604.13888.title: GeoAgentBench: A Dynamic Execution Benchmark for Tool-Augmented Agents in Spatial Analysis
2604.13888.paper_summary: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into Geographic Information Systems (GIS) marks a paradigm shift toward autonomous spatial analysis. However, evaluating these LLM-based agents remains challenging due to the complex, multi-step nature of geospatial workflows. Existing benchmarks primarily rely on static text or code matching, neglecting dynamic runtime feedback and the multimodal nature of spatial outputs. To address this gap, we introduce GeoAgentBench (GABench), a dynamic and interactive evaluation benchmark tailored for tool-augmented GIS agents. GABench provides a realistic execution sandbox integrating 117 atomic GIS tools, encompassing 53 typical spatial analysis tasks across 6 core GIS domains. Recognizing that precise parameter configuration is the primary determinant of execution success in dynamic GIS environments, we designed the Parameter Execution Accuracy (PEA) metric, which utilizes a "Last-Attempt Alignment" strategy to quantify the fidelity of implicit parameter inference. Complementing this, a Vision-Language Model (VLM) based verification is proposed to assess data-spatial accuracy and cartographic style adherence. Furthermore, to address the frequent task failures caused by parameter misalignments and runtime anomalies, we developed a novel agent architecture, Plan-and-React, that mimics expert cognitive workflows by decoupling global orchestration from step-wise reactive execution. Extensive experiments with seven representative LLMs demonstrate that the Plan-and-React paradigm significantly outperforms traditional frameworks, achieving the optimal balance between logical rigor and execution robustness, particularly in multi-step reasoning and error recovery. Our findings highlight current capability boundaries and establish a robust standard for assessing and advancing the next generation of autonomous GeoAI.
2604.13888.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）與地理資訊系統（GIS）的整合標誌著自動化空間分析的範式轉變。<lb/>然而，由於地理空間工作流程的複雜性和多步驟特性，評估這些基於LLM的代理仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>現有的基準主要依賴靜態文本或代碼匹配，忽略了動態運行時反饋和空間輸出的多模態特性。<lb/>為了解決這一差距，我們推出了GeoAgentBench（GABench），這是一個針對工具增強GIS代理的動態和互動評估基準。<lb/>GABench提供了一個現實的執行沙盒，整合了117個原子GIS工具，涵蓋了6個核心GIS領域中的53個典型空間分析任務。<lb/>我們認識到精確的參數配置是動態GIS環境中執行成功的主要決定因素，因此設計了參數執行準確性（PEA）指標，該指標利用“最後嘗試對齊”策略來量化隱式參數推斷的忠實度。<lb/>作為補充，提出了一種基於視覺-語言模型（VLM）的驗證方法，以評估數據空間準確性和地圖風格的一致性。<lb/>此外，為了解決由於參數不對齊和運行時異常引起的頻繁任務失敗，我們開發了一種新的代理架構——計劃與反應，該架構通過將全局協調與逐步反應執行解耦，模擬專家的認知工作流程。<lb/>對七個代表性LLM的廣泛實驗表明，計劃與反應範式顯著超越了傳統框架，在邏輯嚴謹性和執行穩健性之間實現了最佳平衡，特別是在多步推理和錯誤恢復方面。<lb/>我們的研究結果突顯了當前能力的邊界，並為評估和推進下一代自主GeoAI建立了堅實的標準。
2604.13888.author: Bo Yu et.al.
2604.13888.authors: Bo Yu, Cheng Yang, Dongyang Hou, Chengfu Liu, Jiayao Liu, Chi Wang, Zhiming Zhang, Haifeng Li, Wentao Yang
